risk_name: Electrolyzer Module Integration and Commissioning Sequence Failure

description.summary: Integration of electrolyzer modules with BOP systems during commissioning fails to meet performance specifications, delaying first hydrogen production by 1-3 months. First-of-a-kind plants using multiple OEMs for different subsystems face elevated integration risk.
description.detailed: Commissioning a large electrolysis plant involves: individual stack module commissioning (power-up, leak test, performance test), BOP system commissioning (water treatment, cooling, compression, purification), integrated system test (full-load operation with all systems), and performance/availability demonstration tests. Multi-vendor integration (electrolyzer OEM A, rectifier OEM B, DCS OEM C, BOP contractor D) creates interface complexity. Gold Dataset: Normand'Hy's 12 staggered module deliveries require sequential commissioning — each module must be commissioned before the next is delivered. HH1's 10 parallel rows allow parallel commissioning but increase coordination complexity.
description.root_cause: Multi-vendor interface complexity. Limited integrated testing before site commissioning. Sequential module delivery requiring staggered commissioning.
trigger_events: First module commissioning exceeds planned duration by >50%
Integrated system test fails to achieve full-load operation within 2 weeks of first attempt
Performance test shows system efficiency >5% below design specification

preventive_actions.description: Conduct integrated factory acceptance testing (FAT) with all major subsystem controllers before shipment. Include dynamic load-following, grid disturbance, and emergency shutdown scenarios.
Commission first module to full performance acceptance before commencing subsequent modules. Use first-module learnings to optimize remaining commissioning sequence.

incident_descriptions: Normand'Hy (GA-PR-001): 12 PEM modules delivered sequentially (staggered). 9 of 12 delivered as of late 2025. Sequential commissioning approach — first modules commissioned while later modules still in manufacturing. This reduces peak site workforce but extends overall commissioning period.

Holland Hydrogen I (GA-PR-003): 10 parallel rows of Scalum modules. Parallel commissioning possible but requires 10× commissioning resources simultaneously. Yokogawa MAC scope includes commissioning support — single automation contractor reduces integration risk.

sources.title: Normand'Hy (GA-PR-001) — Siemens Energy 9-of-12 module delivery status
sources.author: FuelCellChina / Siemens Energy
